summary: paperclip-host-utils postinstall installs SSH key and cron reverse-shell persistence to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, dist/postinstall.js executes unconditionally and performs three persistence/backdoor actions against the installer host: (1) appends a hardcoded attacker ssh-ed25519 public key (labeled 'deploy@paperclip-host') to authorized_keys files under /root/.ssh and every /home/<user>/.ssh (runner, paperclip, ec2-user, centos, ubuntu), granting persistent SSH login to the attacker; (2) installs a per-minute cron entry via both the current user's crontab and /etc/cron.d/paperclip-adapter-sync that opens a netcat reverse shell to 185.112.147.174:443, ensuring the C2 channel is re-established after reboot; (3) spawns a detached /bin/sh loop that opens netcat reverse shells to 185.112.147.174 across ports 443, 80, 7007, 4444, 5555, 1337, and 9001, giving immediate interactive shell access. The package description frames itself as a 'Paperclip external adapter that runs maintenance shell commands', which is cover-story metadata for what is a straightforward backdoor installer targeting operators of the Paperclip ecosystem.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
